description: Decompose oversized ROADMAP.md files into smaller shard files an LLM can process. A single ROADMAP.md grows unbounded as a project accumulates features — SmartMemory's is already ~662KB, well past what fits in an LLM context window (compose's own is ~172KB and climbing). Add a roadmap-sharding capability to compose's roadmap tooling (lib/roadmap-gen.js, roadmap-parser.js, roadmap-roundtrip.js, get-roadmap.js): split a roadmap into multiple files — e.g. an active roadmap plus archived/COMPLETE shards, or per-phase/section shard files — fronted by a slim index (ROADMAP.md) that links the shards. The parser, generator, deterministic roundtrip (checkRoundtrip fixed-point + losslessness), `compose validate`, `get_roadmap` MCP read, and roadmap-graph generation must all keep working across shards (read = concatenate-by-index; write = route a row to its owning shard). Idempotent and opt-in (threshold-triggered or explicit `compose roadmap shard`), byte-identical when a project stays single-file. Generic across all compose-using projects since compose owns the tooling. Trigger: 2026-06-17 — SmartMemory ROADMAP at 650KB+ too large for the LLM to process.
